threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the bus is a backup plan for the Tube. Real locals know it's the main event, especially with those strikes coming up.
Last Friday, caught a night bus home and a guy was passionately debating whether a kebab counts as a 'proper meal' at 3 AM. Classic Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Sitting on the tram, a group of kids started a spontaneous rap battle. Only in Neukölln would the whole carriage turn into a hype squad. Pure magic.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you witness a crowd cheering as a tram breaks down in the middle of rush hour. It’s a mix of frustration and camaraderie that’s oddly heartwarming.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by the way they know to avoid the Civic Center BART on Fridays. It’s not just the usual delays; it’s chaos and smoke every weekend.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Always amusing how the Muni driver at Civic Center interacts with panhandlers. One called for change, and the driver tossed a peanut butter sandwich out the window.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just for the hipster vibe, but locals know the real treasure lies in the tiny, family-run bakeries hidden in the alleys.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by how they struggle to navigate the Mercat de Sant Antoni—clutching their guidebooks while locals breeze past.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You order a flat white and don’t know the barista's name, but they already remember yours from two visits ago.
Caught the 59 last night; two guys arguing about who won a fight with a pigeon outside Brixton Station. Only in London, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Sitting at the corner café, I watched a guy chase his runaway kebab down the street. He caught it, then shared half with a stray cat. That’s Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find a bus stop where a DJ sets up his turntables at 6 PM. The whole station turns into a mini rave while you wait!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: A true local knows the best spot to dodge the Civic Center BART chaos—while newcomers still gawk at the smoke and sirens.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus today, driver paused mid-ride to let a couple of street performers finish their act. Only in the TL would that happen.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the Sagrada Família is the heart of Barcelona. Real locals know that the true vibe is in the winding streets and small plazas here, like Plaça de la Vila.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always spot them fumbling with the metro map, eagerly asking locals for directions but still managing to end up on the wrong line.
